byNBC 10 NEWS

(WJAR)  —Olympic medalist Elizabeth Beisel is using her clout to encourage young people to donate blood.

Beisel returned to her alma mater on Wednesday morning and held an event at North Kingstown High School.



Olympic medalist Elizabeth Beisel held a rally at North Kingstown High School. to encourage blood donations from youths on May 29, 2024. (WJAR)



She made her pitch to a full auditorium.

“I feel very at home,” she told NBC 10 after the event. “We held a little pep rally to encourage students within age range of 16-24 to donate blood because summer is typically sees a shortage of blood donations.”

Beisel says a blood bank could see about 25% of the typical blood donation amount from teens and  young adults.

“We are here trying to encourage them to build that number back up,” she said.

She said North Kingstown High School has one of the largest drive of donations.



A large crowd of students came out to hear Olympic medalist Elizabeth Beisel speak about blood donation on May 29, 2024. (WJAR)



Being back at the school made Beisel nostalgic.

“So I’m nostalgic being back. So nothing has changed but everything has changed,” she said. “It brings you right back to something that was 14-15 years ago but it was my second home.

The event was in collaboration with the Rhode Island Blood Center.



2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Olympic medalist Elizabeth Beisel encourages youth to donate blood
description: Olympic medalist Elizabeth Beisel is using her clout to encourage young people to donate blood.
